plants.botanical_name: Acer saccharum Green Mountain  SUGAR MAPLE The dark-green leathery foliage of this large (to 70’ x 45’) A.saccharum by A. nigrum hybrid stands up to the hot, dry conditions of summer and turns bright-yellow to scar- let in autumn. S/M (DEC)(z4)(ENa)

Quercus bicolor American Dream  SWAMP OAK Vigorous and very disease resistant; this is broadly pyramidal tree (40' x 50') with glossy green leaves turning a lovely yellow in fall before becoming a fawn brown as they hold onto the tree well into winter.
